Tag (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $54,730,625Details
International Box Office $23,500,000
Worldwide Box Office $78,230,625
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,612,290
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,329,706
Total $7,941,996
Further financial details...

Synopsis

For one month every year, five highly competitive friends hit the ground running in a no-holds-barred game of tag they’ve been playing since the first grade—risking their necks, their jobs and their relationships to take each other down with the battle cry: “You’re It!” This year, the game coincides with the wedding of their only undefeated player, which should finally make him an easy target. But he knows they’re coming…and he’s ready.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,947,396 (27.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.66 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:70.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$28,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,382 opening theaters/3,382 max. theaters, 4.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $71,852,714

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 15th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: June 15th, 2018 (Wide) (Australia)
June 15th, 2018 (Wide), released as Máš ji! (Czech Republic)
June 15th, 2018 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
June 15th, 2018 (Wide), released as Taguj! (Slovakia)
June 15th, 2018 (Wide) (New Zealand)
Video Release: August 28th, 2018 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, crude sexual content, drug use and brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2526 (Cert #51581), 5/9/2018)
Running Time: 100 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Plot point: Delayed Adulthood, Miscellaneous Sports, Wedding Day Disasters

Subgenre: Action Comedy

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

For one month every year, five highly competitive friends hit the ground running in a no-holds-barred game of tag they’ve been playing since the first grade—risking their necks, their jobs and their relationships to take each other down with the battle cry: “You’re It!” This year, the game coincides with the wedding of their only undefeated player, which should finally make him an easy target. But he knows they’re coming…and he’s ready.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$14,947,396 (27.3% of total gross)
